documentation.readme: Simple HS256, HS384 & HS512 JWT token brute force cracker.
usage_sections: Usage: From command line: Where: token: the full HS256-512 JWT token string to crack alphabet: the alphabet to use for the brute force (default: "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789") maxLength: the max length of the string generated during the brute force (de

Example: Cracking the default jwt.io example: It takes about 2 hours in a Macbook Pro (2.5GHz quad-core Intel Core i7). Or using a list of passwords taken from https://github.com/danielmiessler/SecLists It takes less than a second.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the frameworks category. Fits when the engagement needs: Simple HS256, HS384 & HS512 JWT token brute force cracker.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Simple HS256, HS384 & HS512 JWT token brute force cracker.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: npm install --global jwt-cracker. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `jwt-cracker -t <token> [-a <alphabet>] [--max <maxLength>] [-d <dictionaryFilePath>] [-f]`. Further invocations from upstream: `jwt-cracker -t eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiwibmFtZSI6IkpvaG4gRG9lIiwiYWRtaW4iOnRydWV9.TJVA95OrM7E2cBab30RMHrHDcEfxjoYZgeFONFh7HgQ -a abcdefghijklmnopqrstuwxyz --max 6`; `jwt-cracker -t eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiwibmFtZSI6IkpvaG4gRG9lIiwiYWRtaW4iOnRydWV9.TJVA95OrM7E2cBab30RMHrHDcEfxjoYZgeFONFh7HgQ -d darkweb2017-top10000.txt`. Simple HS256, HS384 & HS512 JWT token brute force cracker.

name: jwt-cracker
summary: Simple HS256, HS384 & HS512 JWT token brute force cracker.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use jwt-cracker because Simple HS256, HS384 & HS512 JWT token brute force cracker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `jwt-cracker -t <token> [-a <alphabet>] [--max <maxLength>] [-d <dictionaryFilePath>] [-f]` as documented upstream for jwt-cracker.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `jwt-cracker -t eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiwibmFtZSI6IkpvaG4gRG9lIiwiYWRtaW4iOnRydWV9.TJVA95OrM7E2cBab30RMHrHDcEfxjoYZgeFONFh7HgQ -a abcdefghijklmnopqrstuwxyz --max 6` as documented upstream for jwt-cracker.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `jwt-cracker -t eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiwibmFtZSI6IkpvaG4gRG9lIiwiYWRtaW4iOnRydWV9.TJVA95OrM7E2cBab30RMHrHDcEfxjoYZgeFONFh7HgQ -d darkweb2017-top10000.txt` as documented upstream for jwt-cracker.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair jwt-cracker with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `jwt-cracker --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
